ICGC ARGO (Accelerating Research in Genomic Oncology) generates harmonized cancer genomic and clinical data across international projects.
The legacy ICGC data portal provides somatic mutation and cancer genome data from international consortium projects.
The Clinical Genome Resource curates the clinical relevance of genes and variants for use in precision medicine and research.
The EGA provides controlled-access archiving and distribution of personally identifiable genetic and phenotypic data from biomedical research.
The EMBL-EBI hosts a comprehensive collection of bioinformatics databases and tools for genomics, proteomics, and other omics data.
EMBL-EBI's Expression Atlas provides information on gene and protein expression across tissues, conditions, and species.
EMBL-EBI's Single Cell Expression Atlas provides reanalyzed single-cell RNA-seq expression data across studies.
NHLBI's Trans-Omics for Precision Medicine program generates whole-genome sequencing and omics data linked to heart, lung, blood, and sleep phenotypes.
The Global Alliance for Genomics and Health Beacon protocol enables federated discovery of genetic variants across data resources.
The Human Cell Atlas Data Portal provides standardized single-cell datasets mapping all human cell types.
The Broad Institute's Single Cell Portal hosts, visualizes, and shares single-cell genomics datasets.
recount3 provides uniformly processed RNA-seq gene, exon, and junction counts for human and mouse studies.
The Encyclopedia of DNA Elements project provides comprehensive functional genomics data annotating regulatory elements in the genome.
The NIH Roadmap Epigenomics project provides reference human epigenome maps across cell types and tissues.
The NHGRI-EBI GWAS Catalog is a curated collection of published genome-wide association studies and their SNP-trait associations.
The PheWAS Catalog provides phenome-wide association study results linking genetic variants to a broad range of phenotypes.
Open Targets integrates genetics, omics, and chemical data to support drug target identification and prioritization.
Open Targets Genetics connects GWAS associations with functional genomics to link variants to genes and traits.
DisGeNET is a discovery platform integrating data on gene-disease and variant-disease associations.
Reactome is an open-source, curated database of human biological pathways and reactions.
The Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes integrates genomic, chemical, and systemic functional information into pathway maps.
STRING is a database of known and predicted protein-protein interactions, both physical and functional.
The Molecular Signatures Database provides curated gene sets for gene set enrichment analysis.
The Cancer Dependency Map provides genome-wide CRISPR and RNAi loss-of-function screens and omics across cancer cell lines.
The CCLE provides genomic, transcriptomic, and pharmacologic characterization of human cancer cell lines.
The PDX Network portal shares patient-derived xenograft models and associated genomic and drug-response data.
The Human Protein Atlas maps protein expression across human tissues, cells, and cancers using imaging and omics.
MetaboLights is an EMBL-EBI database for metabolomics experiments and derived information.
The NIH Metabolomics Workbench is a public repository for metabolomics data and metadata across studies.
The IEDB catalogs experimental data on antibody and T-cell epitopes for humans and other species.
10x Genomics provides public single-cell and spatial sequencing datasets for benchmarking and method development.
The CTD curates chemical-gene, chemical-disease, and gene-disease relationships to advance understanding of environmental health.
The Library of Integrated Network-Based Cellular Signatures program generates perturbational omics signatures across cells and conditions.
The Broad Institute Connectivity Map links genes, drugs, and diseases through transcriptional perturbation signatures.
The Global Alliance for Genomics and Health develops standards and frameworks for responsible genomic and health data sharing.
The IHME Global Health Data Exchange catalogs global health and demographic datasets including Global Burden of Disease results.

resource: BioLINCC (Biologic Specimen and Data Repository Information Coordinating Center)
description: NHLBI repository providing access to data and biospecimens from heart, lung, blood, and sleep clinical trials and epidemiological studies for secondary research.
NIH repository sharing de-identified human-subjects data harmonized to a common standard across hundreds of mental-health and neuroscience research projects.
NIDDK repository archiving and distributing clinical data and biospecimens from diabetes, digestive, and kidney disease studies for secondary research.
NICHD repository for storing and sharing de-identified data and biospecimens from studies in child health and human development.
National Institute on Drug Abuse online repository distributing data from completed clinical trials to promote new analyses and research.

resource: PDBP (Parkinson's Disease Biomarkers Program Data Management Resource)
description: NINDS web platform for sharing clinical, imaging, genetic, and biospecimen data across the Parkinson's disease research community.
NINDS-funded biorepository at Indiana University managing and distributing biospecimens for research across more than 25 neurological diseases.

resource: AnVIL (Genomic Data Science Analysis, Visualization, and Informatics Lab-space)
description: NHGRI federated cloud platform for storing, analyzing, and sharing large-scale genomic and related data.

resource: Gabriella Miller Kids First Data Resource Center
description: NIH-funded open cloud-based portal sharing genomic and clinical data on pediatric cancers and structural birth defects.
NIH cloud-enabled repository providing access to curated, de-identified COVID-19 research data harmonized across RADx Initiative studies.
NHLBI cloud-based ecosystem for finding, accessing, sharing, and analyzing large heart, lung, blood, and sleep datasets.
NHLBI-supported repository sharing polysomnography, actigraphy, and questionnaire sleep data from cohorts and clinical trials.
Duke Clinical Research Institute and Bristol Myers Squibb initiative providing researcher access to clinical trial and research datasets.
Web-based functional genomics browser for exploring and visualizing public and private cancer omics data including TCGA, ICGC, and GTEx.
Broad Institute GDAC service providing public access to standardized TCGA multi-platform omics analysis results via web and REST API.
NCI cloud-based repository of harmonized mass-spectrometry tumor proteomic datasets including CPTAC, part of the Cancer Research Data Commons.
NCI cloud-based infrastructure providing a single point of access to genomic, proteomic, imaging, and clinical cancer research data.
NCI cloud-based repository of publicly available, DICOM-standardized cancer imaging data co-located with analysis and exploration tools.
NCI-funded service that de-identifies and publishes cancer-related medical imaging collections with supporting clinical and genomic data.
Cloud-based ecosystem for accessing, analyzing, and visualizing harmonized pediatric cancer and sickle cell genomic data.
Open-access pediatric brain tumor resource providing multi-omic, clinical, imaging, and biospecimen data to researchers.
Global cross-disciplinary registry indexing thousands of research data repositories to help researchers find and deposit data.
Curated registry of databases, standards, and data policies helping researchers locate repositories and meet FAIR data principles.
Free open-source platform from the Center for Open Science for managing, archiving, and sharing research data and materials.
NIH NCATS cloud-based enclave aggregating harmonized EHR data on millions of US patients for COVID-19 and broader observational research.
Community hub for the National COVID Cohort Collaborative describing the data enclave, domain teams, and access process.
Health data ecosystem company providing privacy-preserving patient-level linkage across claims, EHR, and other real-world datasets.
Real-world data and healthcare-AI platform built on a longitudinal Healthcare Map of de-identified US patient encounters.
Cloud platform for discovering, licensing, and linking de-identified medical, pharmacy, and consumer data for life-sciences research.
Real-world evidence software that turns real-world data into transparent, regulatory-grade studies for drug development and access decisions.
Health-outcomes data and AI company providing clinically rich real-world datasets and registries across multiple therapeutic areas.
Precision-medicine company providing molecular and clinical real-world data assets spanning oncology, cardiology, and other specialties.
Real-world oncology evidence company operating a learning health network of provider-sourced precision-medicine patient data.
Collaborative research center whose OptumLabs Data Warehouse links de-identified claims and EHR data on hundreds of millions of US lives.
Dutch population-based network linking pharmacy, GP, hospital, and registry data for pharmacoepidemiology and outcomes research.
European federated network harmonizing data partners across countries to the OMOP common data model for observational research.
Operational portal cataloguing EHDEN data partners and database characterizations across the European OMOP-harmonized network.
Public demonstration instance of the OHDSI ATLAS application for building cohorts and analyses on synthetic OMOP CDM data.
National federated i2b2/ACT network of CTSA sites enabling cohort discovery and feasibility counts across EHR data repositories.
Documentation hub for the Accrual to Clinical Trials federated i2b2/SHRINE network used for multi-site cohort discovery.
Secure analytics platform for running federated studies in situ against tens of millions of NHS primary-care records.
Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services portal for publicly available Medicare, Medicaid, and Marketplace program datasets and tools.
CMS-funded center providing free assistance and documentation for finding, requesting, and using Medicare and Medicaid research data.
Research database linking Medicare and Medicaid beneficiary, claims, and assessment data by beneficiary across the care continuum.
Free online query tool for national and state hospital inpatient and emergency-department statistics from the HCUP databases.
Interactive IHME tool for exploring forecasted mortality, life expectancy, and disease burden scenarios to 2050.
Interactive visualization for comparing Global Burden of Disease estimates across causes, risks, locations, ages, and years.
WHO time-series estimates of mortality, morbidity, life expectancy, and disease burden by country, age, sex, and cause.
CDC Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System, the largest continuous telephone survey of US adult health behaviors and conditions.
NCHS household survey, the oldest ongoing US national health survey covering health status, access, and behaviors.
National Survey on Drug Use and Health, the leading US source of population data on substance use and mental health.

resource: CDC National Center for Health Statistics (NCHS)

NCHS system providing US birth, death, and mortality vital-statistics data and downloadable files for research.
CDC open-data portal offering downloadable and API-accessible public health datasets across surveillance and survey programs.
US federal open-data catalog aggregating high-value health datasets from HHS agencies for public use.
European Commission statistical database of comparable health status, determinants, and healthcare indicators across EU countries.
OECD's comprehensive cross-country dataset on health status, health-care resources, utilization, and expenditure.
Leading scientific resource providing detailed mortality and population data for many developed countries.
Harmonized census and survey microdata collections, including health surveys, integrated across time and geography.
USAID Demographic and Health Surveys providing nationally representative population, health, and nutrition data across 90+ countries.
Census Bureau data dissemination platform with millions of demographic, economic, and population tables and maps.
North American Association of Central Cancer Registries setting registry data standards and aggregating cancer surveillance data.

resource: CDC National Program of Cancer Registries (NPCR)
description: CDC program funding central cancer registries that, with SEER, capture cancer incidence for the entire US population.

resource: US Cancer Statistics Data Visualizations Tool
description: CDC/NCI tool displaying official federal cancer incidence and mortality statistics with state and county breakdowns.
NCI resource describing the linkage of SEER cancer registry data with Medicare claims for outcomes and utilization research.
IARC interactive platform presenting global cancer incidence, mortality, and prevalence statistics (GLOBOCAN).

resource: International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC)
description: WHO cancer agency providing global cancer surveillance data, registries, and epidemiological research resources.
